Title: Neophaseic acid catabolism in the 9′-hydroxylation pathway of abscisic acid in Arabidopsis thaliana

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: NAD(P)-binding Rossmann-fold superfamily; contains conserved YXXXK motif
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key enzyme for neoPA-to-epi-neoDPA conversion in the 9′-hydroxylation branch; broader pathway bottleneck not established
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: reduces neoPA to epi-neoDPA in an NADPH-dependent reaction
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms downstream catabolite of the target metabolite neoPA and modulates ABA homeostasis indirectly
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: ABA catabolism/homeostasis; seed germination regulation through modulation of ABA-related signaling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression increases epi-neoDPA and ABA accumulation and retards seed germination; knockdown/knockout reduces epi-neoDPA and ABA and accelerates seed germination
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NeoPAR1 (AT1G09510) was identified as the Arabidopsis enzyme that converts neoPA into epi-neoDPA in vitro. 2. Tyr163 in the conserved YXXXK motif is essential for catalytic activity. 3. NeoPAR1 overexpression elevates epi-neoDPA and ABA levels and delays seed germination, whereas knockdown/knockout lines show the opposite phenotype. 4. NeoPAR1 therefore contributes to ABA homeostasis through the 9′-hydroxylation catabolic branch.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq-guided candidate screening; recombinant protein enzyme assay in E. coli; LC-MS metabolite profiling; site-directed mutagenesis (Y163A); overexpression; T-DNA knockdown; CRISPR-Cas9 knockout; RT-qPCR; seed germination assay; subcellular localization; promoter-GUS
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Predominantly expressed in dry seeds and siliques; highest around 10-day siliques; also detected in young anthers and chalazal seed coats; expression declines after germination
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Predominantly expressed in dry seeds and siliques; highest around 10-day siliques; also detected in young anthers and chalazal seed coats; expression declines after germination
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: seed germination assays with or without 2-day stratification; neoPA application; growth at 23°C and 32°C in germination tests
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S constitutive promoter for overexpression and YFP localization; native NeoPAR1 promoter for GUS reporter
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to tune seed germination and ABA homeostasis by editing or modulating expression; reduced NeoPAR1 may accelerate germination, whereas overexpression may enhance dormancy-like traits; caution that ABA levels are also altered
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Changes in ABA homeostasis may affect dormancy and other ABA-dependent processes beyond germination
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: NeoPAR1 also shows very weak PA reductase activity (~0.3% of ABH2 activity) but CRL1 cannot convert neoPA; neoPA retains partial ABA-like activity whereas epi-neoDPA is inactive
